reviews.review_title: Perfect for Developers and Focused Work
reviews.review_description:
I only downloaded Immersed recently and I’ve already upgraded to Pro because it is genuinely brilliant.

As a developer, I usually have loads of apps, tabs, code editors, browsers, dashboards and design tools open at the same time. Immersed gives me the extra screen space I need without needing a huge physical desk setup. Having multiple virtual screens makes my workflow feel so much cleaner, and I can keep everything organised on its own display.

What I love most is the peace and focus it gives me. The virtual surroundings help me block out distractions and get properly locked into my development work. It feels like having my own private workspace wherever I am.

The additional screens are perfect for coding, testing, designing and managing projects all at once. For anyone who works with multiple apps open, especially developers, designers or remote workers, this app is 100% worth trying.

I fully rate Immersed and would definitely recommend it. Trust me, if you are a developer like me, it is worth it. 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

Hate to say it but I regret buying Virtual Desktop. Immersed works so much better and is free, and supports direct USB link. Set your own Custom360 Environment and you can just disappear and get some work done undisturbed until your Quest3 battery runs out. I'd get the 4th and 5th monitors running but my humble Macbook Neo can't handle it.

reviews.review_title: 5/5 Stars – Immersed is a Game-Changer f
reviews.review_description:
I’ve been using Immersed on my Meta Quest 3 days now, and it has genuinely transformed how I work. If you’re tired of a cluttered desk or multiple physical monitors, this app lets you spawn up to 5 massive virtual screens in stunning VR environments—no extra hardware required. It works seamlessly with Windows, Mac, and Linux.
￼
What I Love Most:
•  Multiple High-Quality Screens: I can easily set up a main coding window, reference docs, Slack/Teams, browser tabs, and even a vertical screen for notes. The ability to resize, curve, reposition, and adjust resolutions makes it incredibly flexible. Text is sharp enough for full-day productivity (especially on Quest 3).
•  Immersive Environments: Working in a cozy virtual café, a sleek modern office, or a scenic distraction-free space keeps me hyper-focused for hours. The mixed-reality passthrough portals for my physical keyboard and mouse are brilliant—you stay in VR but can still type naturally. ￼
•  Collaboration Features: Screen sharing, whiteboarding, and joining the same virtual room with teammates feel natural. The VR webcam turns you into your Meta avatar for meetings, which is both fun and professional.
•  Wireless & Portable: Fully wireless over Wi-Fi (strong connection recommended). I can work from anywhere without being chained to my desk.
•  Free Tier is Generous: You get solid functionality right away (multiple screens, public co-working), and the Pro upgrade unlocks the full 5 screens and higher resolutions at a reasonable price. ￼
It’s perfect for programmers, writers, designers, students, or anyone who benefits from screen real estate and deep focus. I’ve had marathon sessions where I lose track of time because distractions are minimized and everything feels right there.
Minor nitpicks: Occasional connection hiccups if your Wi-Fi isn’t great, and long sessions can still cause some headset warmth/sweat (better with a good head strap and fan). Battery life means you’ll want to plug in for all-day use, but that’s standard for VR.
Overall, Immersed is the best productivity app I’ve tried in VR. It makes working feel novel, efficient, and even enjoyable. If you have a Quest headset, download it now—you’ll wonder how you ever worked without it. Highly recommended! 
— Cesar, Cape Coral, FL

reviews.review_title: Awesome to use in limited spaces
reviews.review_description:
Only began using the application but already loving it for a convenient workflow and can see myself using it more in the future for working while traveling.

I definitely feel more efficient having more screens to work with and this provides a nice balance of not having to physically set up another monitor somewhere.

reviews.review_title: Extremely Better than Meta Quest Remote
reviews.review_description:
I'm having a much softer experience with Immersed on MacOS.

It's way more customizable, as you can not only resize and move windows around your workspace, but also rotate them to vertical position, make them curved the way you like, and they're much better positioned than other apps, like Meta Quest Remote Desktop. 

Meta Quest RD kept freezing and had the worst resolution. 

Furthermore, you can have up to 5 screens and they keep anchored. 

Loving it. Simply loving it....

Saying anything is “life changing” feel hyperbolic but it’s not untrue in this situation. As someone who is diagnosed with ADHD and trying to find ways to be productive, immersed has given me a serious boost.
Just installed this app and working with a MAC remote and what a change in productivity for me.  Great App and saved me a lot of time

Integrated my peripherals easily with different options available. It also remembers your screen positions.  
There are many environments available and also public ones you can encounter other people and still remain private.

reviews.review_title: A VR workspace that just works!
reviews.review_description:
A buddy of mine turned me onto Immersed because he works from home and needed more screen space, and he was absolutely right.

I’ve been using it at my dining room table whenever I’ve got screen-heavy work to do. Case in point: right now I’m doing my taxes with it. I’ve got three screens up: receipts on one, tax software on another, and music on a third. It just works.

The interface is super clean, and the cutout feature is a game changer. I can see my keyboard and mouse (which you definitely want), plus I can add cutouts for things like pets, people, or even a wall clock or TV. It makes working in VR feel way more natural.

Let’s be real, there are other options out there. I’ve tried a few. Every time I do, I end up thinking, “why isn’t this like Immersed?” At this point, I don’t even bother switching anymore. I’m sticking with what works.

Bonus: I bought a laptop last year with a smaller screen than I wanted… doesn’t matter at all anymore when I’m using Immersed with my Quest.

Give it a shot. I bet  you’ll like it!

Downloaded this for the first time yesterday, using Quest 3. Very straightforward to connect to my iMac, now have three massive virtual screens to work on! I was disappointed that Apple stopped making the 27” iMacs and have been working on a 24” for the last year as their pro screens are ridiculously expensive! Now I have the equivalent of three and as large as I want them to be. 😁Tried in VR and MR; both are amazing ways to use this app. 
I love how you can also network with other users around the world, and co-work. Working from home can be isolating sometimes! This app has opened up new and better ways of working for me. I am now excited to be able to finally work in VR/MR - I am an Augmented Reality Digital/Print Designer, I can stay immersed to use all my work programs without having to come in and out of the headset. From 3D modelling in Gravity Sketch, to all Adobe Creative Cloud apps. This is just brilliant, thanks devs you are absolutely amazing 💯 x 💯 🌟 🌟 🌟 🌟 🌟 🤩
i used immersed years ago and it has come so far since then. this is the promise of what vr is supposed to be.

Just out of curiousity, i tried joining my online Microsoft Teams classes on my Quest 2 on the browser... lets just say the lag was not the best and the browser kept crashing. After searching up immersed, i was impressed. The fact that it runs more smoothly than steam link and other apps i used i was super satisfied especially since its free. My user is m7x11

I hate Immersed. I must have rage quit and uninstalled it a dozen times since Oct 2023. The worthless developers can't even understand how much easier it would be to grab and rotate screens by grabbing them from the very center using the grab button. They can't even fix the simplest bug. User avatars are always floating in the air or stuck in the floor. Even the built-in 'Start with Windows' option doesn't even work. The UI is hideous and disorganized, and there are too many freaking popup notifications. Immersed always requires you to have two controllers by you at all times, taking up valuable desk space. But Virtual Desktop will let you do anything and everything with just one controller. Immersed also banned me on Discord for no reason, even though I was barely active on there and made very few posts. I stubbornly refuse to ever install this app again. I use Virtual Desktop without the multi user experience and it uses way less battery life. Beware all these fake positive reviews. You can tell the reviews are fake by the way they are worded and the fact that Immersed is completely dead with no users.
this app is great for hanging out with my friends watching stuff

burns through my Quest 3 battery way too fast and leaves it very hot, even though i only have 3 desktops and am not co-working. screen resolutions are terrible and blurry. annoying popups kill productivity, and the connection constantly freezes without warning, leading me to disconnect and connect to my mac over and over again. also hand tracking gets too sensitive or unresponsive at the worst of times - jumps all over my face when i move my mouse, and takes forever to open menu when i need it. just use meta remote desktop
Works smooth. No lag!
Super app
Ican work and lookfreely
The future is now. This is amazing app allows you to work on PC. Coding in this mode is fantastic.

reviews.review_description: so usually I'm on remote desktop because its more convenient natively to use, but they don't have more than 1 screen, which is when I start to use Immersed, so very specific use case. I like the environments too but typically i just use the mixed reality version.
Finally a good desktop streaming app that actually works on Linux. Thank you very much!

The ability to surround yourself with multiple virtual screens in VR is something you have to experience to fully appreciate. Try it for free, what have you got to lose?

reviews.review_description: One of the most exciting apps. Working with multiple display is such a cool thing and Immersed makes it look so good!

I love this app. I have tried them all too. This app has all the features you are looking for. The screens are sharp and crisp. you can place them where you want and collaborate with friends and co workers. I know I sound like a commercial or something but this app is everything you are looking for in one place!!

That`s quite a wonderful surprise! Strongly recommend the app !🏁🌅🙌🎯

Does what it is supposed to, very nice, but selling "backgroud images" , no preview,  for about $25/piece, while selling "credits" in like "$20" sizes, is just directly, greedy, and does not exactly feel like a company you can trust,

This app is super helpful for me with ADHD. I made a rule that whenever I am using Immersed, I can only be doing actual work on my computer. When I'm using it, I don't get distracted by the room around me (clutter or whatever). Also, a great benefit (at least for me) is that I don't need my glasses when I'm in the headset. I'm farsighted (nearsightedness was fixed with LASIK almost 20 years ago), and it seems that my eyes see what's in the headset as if I'm looking off in the distance, even though the lenses are right in front of my eyes. That means that my eyes are more relaxed while doing computer tasks than they would be if I were wearing my glasses looking at the actual screen. YMMV.
It works super well for work and things, i can have 5 monitors as big as i need them instead of buying them all!

reviews.review_title: Using for years, this is the best
reviews.review_description: The ease of arranging window, changing sizes of screens, and effectively working from vr is simply the best on immersed. No other app even comes close to them. I do wish they could add more new variety in the room options, but im cherry picking. Use this with a cable for best performance!
The app is really cool and handy for working with multiple screens, but you cannot last to long. You will get overwhelmed in like an hour. No pretentions to the app, it's about the headset itsself
easily the best app for productivity. seamlessly works with my lenovo legion go to turn a small factor tablet like windows machine into a powerful five monitor workstation. love this program
The app works great for focused work, multiple screens and travel.

1: required accounts should not be needed for local access alone. Pairing worked then nothing worked after the tutorial. No “screen reset” when they floated out of FOV

2: microtransaction hell is for mobile games, not productivity apps.

The app has become way less useful over the years - especially now that HMDs have virtual screen streaming and collaboration being built into the spatial OS or as simpler overlay apps that can be stacked.  Stopped using Immersed when they were limited to having 1 physical display, meaning you had to unplug your monitors.  Not long after, they turned on the commuity and started giving away data to third party companies.

As a cherry on top, they've been openly misleading customers and investors in relation to their new Visor headset.

I'd be wary of installing Desktop PC software from a company that acts as shady as Immersed has since their recent Visor event failures and lack of funds/revenue.  It's no telling at this point what they're trying to harvest from your machine or network to try to make a buck.

If you're like me and use your Meta Quest to tune out the world and focus in on work this is the perfect app. The free version is all you need but simply using it will earn you credits for the Pro version. You may need to tinker under the hood now and then to get it set up the way you want it but there's a great Discord community for help and the developers are extremely responsive. It's the app I use whenever I put on my headset even though my kids ridicule me.

The environments are beautiful and they are helping me to isolate and focus. In general the app works nicely and seems well thought. Also there is a nice cutout that lets you see the keyboard and the mouse.
Have tried a few and this one is my favorite

Immersed genuinely changed how I use VR with my PC. Being able to spawn multiple virtual monitors, resize them, and work comfortably for long sessions is way more practical than I expected. Performance has been solid for me, text clarity is good, and the customization (environments, monitor layout, passthrough options) makes it feel tailored rather than gimmicky.

I especially like how it integrates with real workflows instead of just being a novelty. 10/10 would use again
Excellent productivity app.. The reason i bought my quest 3 tbh...
I did not think my office work will be this much fun with Immersed. Loved it
Fun and reliable i love this app free to use i do recommend
Works flawlessly in all areas

Tested on Linux (Wayland) + Quest 3.

The input and spatial interaction model is fundamentally broken.

– The virtual keyboard does not generate usable key events on Linux, making text input impossible without a physical keyboard.
– Pointer interaction suffers from depth and collision issues, causing the cursor to hit invisible barriers or register behind virtual surfaces.
– Screens and the virtual keyboard cannot be freely positioned in 3D space. Their movement is restricted to predefined arcs and zones, with no proper 6DOF interaction.  (yes really, how you got an idea to limit virtual keyboard to left side of screen<->center ????) 

These are not minor bugs but deliberate design limitations that remove user control over spatial layout.
As a result, the workspace cannot be adapted to the user’s body, posture, or desk setup.

This is not a true VR workspace, but a constrained virtual monitor system.

reviews.review_title: Immersed VR is hands down the best

The interface is intuitive, setup is seamless, and the overall experience makes multitasking and productivity in virtual reality truly enjoyable.  Highly recommended!
